app.title: Open MPP Explorer
app.description: On-chain session funding and channel lifecycle on Tempo (MPP / TempoStreamChannel)

nav.closeNavAria: Payer channels: withdraw-ready, grace period, or request close — read-only indexer lists

search.aria: Search by address or channel ID
search.quickSearchHint: Choose Payer or Payee, then enter an address for that role’s channel list. A 0x… + 64 hex value opens the channel detail page.

session.termShort: Here, “session” means the on-chain footprint of MPP Session flows: escrow in a TempoStreamChannel, settlement, and finality—not each off-chain HTTP payment step.
session.termTooltip: On-chain unit is the payment channel (channelId). Incremental vouchers and HTTP 402 flows are off-chain and are not shown in this explorer.

analytics.bucketUtcNote: Bucket starts are shown in UTC (as returned by the indexer).
analytics.applyRangeToSummary: Apply date range to summary (events & settlement in window)
analytics.rangeHint: Enable “Apply date range to summary” and pick dates to load in-window event totals and settlement volume.
analytics.timeseriesNeedDates: Set From and To to load buckets.
analytics.breakdownContractNeedChain: Select a chain in the filter bar (or ensure chain metadata loaded) to load contract breakdown.
analytics.indexerNotRealtime: Figures reflect the indexer, not necessarily the live chain tip. Blocks and channel rows can lag behind real-time.
analytics.indexerSyncErrorShort: Could not load per-chain synced heights from /meta/sync; KPIs may still show a summary height when the API provides it.
analytics.indexerSyncSelectedChain: Indexer processed through block {{height}} on chain {{chain}} (from /meta/sync).

glossary.amountsNote: Token amounts (escrow, settlement deltas, channel ranking totals) are displayed as fixed-point values with 6 fractional digits (same on-chain convention as TIP-20 balances in these APIs). Counts (channels, events) are integers.
glossary.remainingEscrowBody: Sum of funds still locked in channels that are not finalized, grouped by token contract address. A channel is “open” from the indexer’s perspective until it reaches a finalized state in the indexed data.
glossary.settlementVolumeBody: In a time window, sum of settlement deltas across indexed channel updates (same units as on-chain settlement accounting—typically the channel token). The optional token filter on the settlement time series restricts sums to one token.
glossary.bucketUtcBody: Time-series rows are grouped into hour or day buckets. The bucket label is the interval start in UTC returned by the backend.
glossary.eventsByNameBody: Counts of indexed contract events in a bucket or summary window, keyed by Solidity event name from TempoStreamChannel (and related logs the indexer tracks).
glossary.syncedHeightBody: Latest block height the indexer has successfully processed for a chain (from /meta/sync or embedded in summary). Use it as a freshness bound—data above this height is not reflected yet.
glossary.rankingMetricBody: Channel leaderboard rows sort by either settled amount or deposit (API field), both interpreted as token fixed-point amounts with 6 fractional digits for display.

detail.title: Channel Detail
detail.onChainStorageNote: Channel state and events come from the indexer (on-chain contracts). Finalization and balances shown here reflect what is stored and derived from the chain, not off-chain HTTP payment steps.
detail.eventsSummaryEmpty: No event statistics for this channel yet.

actionable.inputHint: Enter a Payer address to view actionable channels

channels.copyPageTsvAria: Copy current page of channels as tab-separated values

home.title: Overview
home.subtitle: Tempo channels and on-chain session analytics — read-only explorer.
home.syncStripNote: Heights reflect the indexer, not necessarily the live chain tip.
home.indexerFreshnessAria: Indexer sync height and data freshness (from /meta/sync)
home.chartsIntro: Daily buckets, last 7 days, all chains — same default window as Analytics (day).

channelsList.title: All channels
channelsList.subtitle: Paginated current state for every indexed channel. Click a row for detail: lifecycle events and balance evolution.
channelsList.seoDescAll: Paginated list of all indexed TempoStreamChannel rows on Tempo—deposits, status, payer and payee, for MPP Session flows.
channelsList.seoDescFinalized: Paginated history of finalized TempoStreamChannel contracts on Tempo—payment channel outcomes after settlement.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Network-level KPIs, time series, rankings, and indexer sync. Filters sync with the URL for sharing.
analytics.seoDescription: On-chain session analytics for MPP / TempoStreamChannel on Tempo: active and finalized channels, escrow by token, time series, rankings, contract and token breakdowns, and indexer sync—read-only.
analytics.phaseBNote: For per-channel inspection and paginated lists, use

actionable.title: Close & withdraw (payer)
actionable.subtitle: Indexed TempoStreamChannel rows only: no wallet, no transactions. Lists follow /channels/actions/* APIs.

analyticsPartner.seoDescription: Address-scoped analytics for TempoStreamChannel on Tempo: open and finalized counts, remaining escrow, and (for payers) event volume buckets—indexer-backed, read-only.
analyticsPartner.payerTsLimitHint: If the backend returns an error about too many rows, narrow the date range or select a chain.

guide.title: Open MPP Explorer — Guide & FAQ
guide.intro: How MPP Session flows on Tempo use TempoStreamChannel for escrow and settlement, what this site shows (on-chain only), and how to navigate — organized as short questions.
guide.onchainTitle: On-chain only — no off-chain HTTP payment UI
guide.onchainLead: This explorer reflects indexed blockchain facts: channel rows, contract events, and derived balances. It does not display HTTP 402 challenges, Payment-Authorization headers, per-request vouchers, SSE receipts, or other off-chain session mechanics. Single API “sessions” are not replayable here unless their effects appear as channel state or on-chain events.
guide.tempoP1b: is a Layer 1 built for payments at scale (including stablecoins and machine payments).
guide.tempoP2b: defines machine-to-machine payments (e.g. per API call or tool use). In this product, “MPP Sessions” refer to flows that move value through TempoStreamChannel on Tempo.
guide.tempoP3: Channels can be opened and topped up, then reused while open—like prepaid escrow—with settlement deferred until close or finality.
guide.howOverview: Overview — KPIs (active / finalized counts, remaining escrow) and quick search.
guide.howChannels: Channels — full paginated list of indexed channels.
guide.howAnalytics: Analytics — network KPIs, UTC-bucket time series, rankings, token and contract breakdowns, indexer sync; filters stay in the URL.
guide.howSearch: Header or Overview search — payer address or 66-character channel ID (0x + 64 hex). For payee-scoped lists, use the path /address/payee/ with the payee address.
guide.howDetail: Channel detail — payer, payee, token, deposits, status, lifecycle events, balance evolution; links to the block explorer when available.
guide.howActionable: Close (nav) / payer — lists channels that are withdraw-ready, in grace, or can request close (read-only indexer /channels/actions/*).
guide.howFinalized: Channels — switch to Finalized or open /channels?finalized=1; data from GET /channels?c_finalized=1&sort=c_updated_block.
guide.readOnly: The app is read-only: no wallet connection and no transaction broadcast.
guide.settlementBody: While a channel is open, value moves under protocol rules without every micro-update on-chain. Settlement aligns with lifecycle: close or expiry brings agreed balances on-chain; a grace period may apply after a close request. Rules come from Tempo and the contracts; the UI shows indexed status, events, and balances.

faq.qScope: What appears on this explorer—and what is intentionally omitted?
faq.qTempoMpp: What are Tempo, MPP, and TempoStreamChannel?
faq.aTempoMpp: <tempo>Tempo</tempo> is a Layer 1 built for payments at scale (including stablecoins and machine payments). <mpp>MPP (Machine Payments Protocol)</mpp> defines machine-to-machine payments (e.g. per API call or tool use). In this product, "MPP Sessions" refer to flows that move value through TempoStreamChannel on Tempo.
faq.qChannelModel: How do channels work in simple terms?
faq.qOverview: What does the Overview (home page) show?
faq.qChannels: Where is the channel list—and finalized-only history?
faq.qSearch: How do I search by address or channel ID?
faq.qDetail: What does a channel detail page include?
faq.qActionable: What does the Close (payer) navigation entry show?
faq.qFinalized: How do I browse finalized channels?
faq.qReadOnly: Can I connect a wallet or broadcast transactions here?
faq.qSettlement: How does settlement relate to what I see on-chain?

footer.formalBrand: Open MPP Explorer — read-only Tempo channel & MPP session data
